title: GPT 5.6 Sol Just Blew Up The AI World
summary: OpenAI launched GPT 5.6 Sol with limited access to trusted partners following U.S. government pressure. The model features new coding and cybersecurity capabilities. OpenAI also revealed Jalapeño, its first custom inference chip co-designed with Broadcom.
writeup: The video covers two OpenAI announcements: GPT 5.6 Sol, released as a limited preview after government pressure and pitched around new coding and cyber capabilities, and Jalapeño, a custom inference chip built with Broadcom. The chip matters because it signals a move to reduce reliance on Nvidia and own more of the AI stack. The takeaways: preview access to the new model is restricted, its emphasis is on coding and cyber work, and OpenAI is pursuing custom silicon for inference.
